added: Inline SVG lifecycle diagram (introduced -> discovered -> disclosed -> patched -> remediated) with a bracket marking the window of exposure, placed inside the 'How a vulnerability becomes a CVE' section right after the paragraph that defines that term

Inline SVG pipeline diagram showing artifact -> build inventory -> (match advisories | check licenses) -> report/SBOM, emphasising that both checks read the same inventory, placed at the end of the 'How SCA works, step by step' section

Inline SVG matching-logic diagram: rule sections (meta/strings/condition) -> per-string scan results -> condition evaluation -> finding contents, making the point that the condition decides the verdict rather than the raw hit count; placed at the end of 'How YARA Rules Work'

Inline SVG six-step exploit-chain diagram (attacker input -> logged -> lookup expanded -> JNDI resolves -> malicious class reference -> JVM executes) with a bracket marking where upgrading to 2.17.1 or removing JndiLookup cuts the chain; placed at the end of 'How the exploit worked'

Inline SVG regression timeline banding OpenSSH version ranges (pre-4.4p1 vulnerable, 4.4p1-8.4p1 safe, 8.5p1-9.7p1 vulnerable again, 9.8p1+ fixed) against the 2006 fix, the October 2020 commit 752250c reintroduction, and the 1 July 2024 disclosure; placed after the Affected Versions list
